A Haunting in Venice (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $42,471,412Details
International Box Office $79,819,044
Worldwide Box Office $122,290,456
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $990,166
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,220,985
Total $2,211,151
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In post-World War II Venice, Poirot, now retired and living in his own exile, reluctantly attends a séance. But when one of the guests is murdered, it is up to the former detective to once again uncover the killer.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,279,529 (33.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.97 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:34.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,305 opening theaters/3,305 max. theaters, 5.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $46,431,267

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 15th, 2023 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
International Releases: September 13th, 2023 (Wide) (France)
September 13th, 2023 (Wide) (Belgium)
September 13th, 2023 (Wide) (Indonesia)
September 13th, 2023 (Wide), released as A Haunting in Venice (베니스 유령 살인사건) (South Korea)
September 13th, 2023 (Wide) (Philippines)
Video Release: October 31st, 2023 by 20th Century Studios
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some strong violence, disturbing images and thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2786 (cert #54491), 6/7/2023)
Running Time: 103 minutes
Franchise: Kenneth Branagh's Hercule Poirot
Filming location (country): England, Italy, United Kingdom
Filming location (production facility): Pinewood Studios, Buckinghamshire, England
Plot point: Murder Mystery, Post War, Séance
Subgenre: Mystery Drama
Time period setting: 1940s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Studios
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
